additional_employment_experience___posit.name_and_address_of_employer_1:
Harborview Community Health Clinic
4120 Westlake Ave N
Seattle, WA 98109
additional_employment_experience___posit.type_of_business_1: Nonprofit community health clinic providing primary care and social services
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_line_1_1: Served as the primary front-desk point of contact for patients, checking them in and out for appointments, verifying insurance information, collecting co-pays, and ensuring that all demographic and contact information was accurate and updated in the electronic health record (EHR) system (Epic).
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_line_2_1: Coordinated appointment scheduling for a team of eight primary care providers and two behavioral health counselors, managing a high-volume phone line (averaging 60–80 calls per day), triaging urgent requests, and working closely with medical assistants and nurses to fit in same-day and walk-in visits when clinically appropriate.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_line_3_1: Provided detailed information to patients—many of whom were uninsured, underinsured, or non-native English speakers—about available financial assistance programs, sliding-scale fees, and community resources; frequently helped patients complete application forms for charity care and state benefits, and followed up by phone to ensure documentation was received and processed.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_line_4_1: Maintained accurate daily cash logs, balanced the cash drawer at the end of each shift, generated visit and billing reports, and collaborated with the billing department to resolve discrepancies; also trained two new front-desk staff members on clinic workflows, customer service expectations, and privacy/confidentiality policies (HIPAA).
additional_employment_experience___posit.reason_for_leaving_1: Relocated to another city due to spouse’s new job opportunity.
additional_employment_experience___posit.reason_for_leaving_additional_line_1: My spouse was offered a promotion with her company that required us to relocate from Seattle to Portland on relatively short notice in late summer 2020. Because Harborview Community Health Clinic primarily serves the local community and does not have locations outside the Seattle area, there was no option to transfer internally. I discussed the situation with my supervisor, Angela Ramirez, about two months in advance, and we mutually agreed on an end date that allowed me to help train my replacement and document front-desk procedures so the transition would be as smooth as possible for both staff and patients. I left on good terms, received a positive written reference, and have remained in occasional contact with Angela for networking and professional advice.

additional_employment_experience___posit.name_and_address_of_employer_2:
GreenLeaf Natural Foods Market
2875 NE Fremont St
Portland, OR 97212
additional_employment_experience___posit.type_of_business_2: Independent neighborhood grocery store specializing in organic and locally sourced foods
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_2_line_1: Provided day-to-day customer service on the sales floor and at the front registers, assisting customers with locating products, explaining differences between organic and conventional items, and answering questions about ingredients, allergens, and sourcing, with a particular focus on local vendors and seasonal produce.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_2_line_2: Operated cash registers and point-of-sale systems, processed cash, credit, EBT, and check transactions, handled returns and exchanges in accordance with store policy, and ensured accurate balancing of my drawer at the end of each shift; as a shift lead, I also performed closing procedures, including safe counts and deposit preparation.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_2_line_3: Helped coordinate weekly product displays and endcaps, especially for new local products and promotional items, by collaborating with department leads to create visually appealing and informative signage; occasionally assisted the grocery buyer with basic inventory tasks such as cycle counts and restocking high-turnover items.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_2_line_4: Trained and mentored several new hires on customer service standards, register operations, and store policies, emphasizing clear communication, patience with customers who had dietary restrictions, and a friendly, community-oriented approach; also participated in monthly staff meetings where we discussed ways to reduce food waste and improve the overall customer experience.
additional_employment_experience___posit.reason_for_leaving_2: Accepted a position with better pay, benefits, and alignment with long-term career goals in healthcare administration.
additional_employment_experience___posit.reason_for_leaving_2_additional_line: While I genuinely enjoyed working at GreenLeaf—especially the community feel and the relationships I built with regular customers—I was seeking a role that offered more stable hours, health benefits, and clear opportunities for professional growth. When the Patient Services Coordinator position at Harborview Community Health Clinic opened up, it aligned much more closely with my interest in healthcare and community services. The new role offered full-time hours, employer-sponsored health insurance, and the chance to work directly with medical staff and patient records, which I felt would be a stronger foundation for my long-term goal of moving into healthcare administration or clinic operations management. I gave GreenLeaf a full four weeks’ notice, helped train two new cashiers, and assisted with updating some of the opening and closing checklists before my departure to ensure a smooth handoff.

additional_employment_experience___posit.name_and_address_of_employer_3:
Northwest Literacy Partners
Volunteer Services Office
615 SW 3rd Ave, Suite 410
Portland, OR 97204
additional_employment_experience___posit.type_of_business_3: Nonprofit organization focused on adult literacy and English language tutoring
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_3_line_1: Met one-on-one with adult learners twice per week at the downtown library and community centers to provide individualized tutoring in reading comprehension, basic writing skills, and practical literacy tasks such as filling out forms, reading medication labels, and understanding workplace safety materials.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_3_line_2: Collaborated with the Volunteer Coordinator to develop informal lesson plans tailored to each learner’s goals and starting skill level, using a combination of organization-provided curriculum, library resources, and real-world materials (e.g., bus schedules, job applications, and news articles) to keep sessions relevant and engaging.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_3_line_3: Maintained detailed notes after each session documenting what we covered, areas where the learner struggled, and any homework assignments given; shared updates with staff during quarterly volunteer check-ins so that overall program progress could be tracked and additional resources provided where needed.
additional_employment_experience___posit.describe_your_duties_3_line_4: Participated in periodic volunteer training workshops on topics such as working with English language learners, cultural sensitivity, trauma-informed approaches, and supporting adults who may feel embarrassed about their reading level; applied these techniques to build trust and encourage learners to advocate for themselves at work, in healthcare settings, and with their children’s schools.
additional_employment_experience___posit.reason_for_leaving_3: Ended regular volunteer commitment due to schedule changes and increased work hours at paid employment, but remained on the organization’s email list for occasional events and future volunteer opportunities.
